title: About
text:
Abdelkarim el Ghani is a Research Assistant at the Erasmus School of Social and Behavioural Sciences (ESSB), Erasmus University Rotterdam. His expertise lies in statistical data analysis, neuroscience, and open science practices, with a strong focus on R programming, data visualization, and managing large-scale research datasets.
Abdelkarim has extensive experience in both academic and clinical research environments. He has contributed to international conferences, authored scientific publications, and supported open science initiatives by sharing code and workflows on platforms like the Open Science Framework. His research interests include neurodevelopment, brain imaging, and statistical modeling of behavioral data.
Driven by a passion for data-driven problem-solving, Abdelkarim aims to advance reproducible and transparent scientific research.

title: Rotterdam Gold
text:
Project Overview
Evaluate longitudinal survey data to understand the impact of school interventions (“focus” vs. “control” schools) on student well-being and coping strategies across four waves.


Key Steps

Data Acquisition: Imported SPSS datasets for four waves.
Data Cleaning: Standardized school names and grouped them into focus/control categories.
Construct Index Calculation: Created indices for optimism, self-efficacy, and coping strategies.
Exploratory Data Analysis: Assessed sample sizes and summarized variables.
Results Communication: Visualized differences and prepared recommendations.


# Create a fake dataset for demonstration
set.seed(123)
survey_data <- data.frame(
  school_id = rep(c("A", "B", "C", "D"), each = 50),
  student_id = 1:200,
  OPTIMISM_1 = rnorm(200, mean = 3.5, sd = 0.5),
  OPTIMISM_7 = rnorm(200, mean = 3.6, sd = 0.5),
  school_group = rep(c("focus", "control"), each = 100)
)

Constructing Indices:

# Optimism Index Example
survey_data <- survey_data %>%
  mutate(W1_OPTIMISM = rowMeans(select(., starts_with("OPTIMISM_")), na.rm = TRUE))

Visualizing Results:

# Boxplot comparing focus and control groups
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(survey_data, aes(x = school_group, y = W1_OPTIMISM)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightblue") +
  labs(title = "Optimism Index by School Group", x = "Group", y = "Optimism Index")








Outcome: - Insights supported intervention strategies by highlighting differences in well-being between focus and control schools. - Recommendations were provided to improve program effectiveness and data collection.






Publication



Rotterdam Goud: Eindrapport Pilot Rotterdams Goud: Wijzen van leren en sociaal-emotioneel welbevinden. Dijkstra, K., & el Ghani, A., (2024).
